help.personal: Enter your personal information: first name, last name, target job title, email, phone, address and city. You can add your LinkedIn or website. Photo is optional but gives a more professional impression.
help.profile:
Write a professional summary of 3-5 sentences describing who you are, your experience and key skills. This is the first thing a recruiter will read — be clear and compelling.

Example: "Software engineer with 4 years of web development experience. Specialized in React and Node.js, passionate about innovative technical solutions."
help.exp:
Add each job you've held, starting with the most recent. For each: job title, company name, city, start and end date.

In 'Responsibilities & achievements', describe what you did and accomplished. Use action verbs: 'Managed...', 'Developed...', 'Increased...'
help.edu:
Add your academic degrees and important certifications. For each: degree/diploma name, institution name, city and graduation year.

Start with your highest degree.
help.skills:
Enter your professional soft skills: team management, negotiation, time management, analysis, creativity, problem solving...

Rate each skill from 1 (beginner) to 5 (expert) with the green dots.
help.tools:
Enter software and digital tools you're proficient in: Microsoft Word, Excel, PowerPoint, AutoCAD, Photoshop, Python, SQL...

Rate your proficiency from 1 to 5.
help.langs:
Enter languages you speak and rate your level:

⭐ Beginner
⭐⭐ Intermediate
⭐⭐⭐ Fluent
⭐⭐⭐⭐ Bilingual
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ Native
help.interests: Add your hobbies and personal interests (reading, sports, photography, cooking...). This section is optional but adds a human dimension to your CV and helps create positive connection points with the recruiter.
